name: AI Assisted Invoice Processor
summary: An application to help finance teams collect invoices, extract structured data, review exceptions, and move approved records into downstream reporting without juggling disconnected inboxes, sheets, and shared folders.
highlights: Built for finance workflow automation around invoice collection, extraction, review, and downstream reporting.
manual_highlights: Built the frontend with React.js, Next.js, Tailwind CSS, Lucide React, and TanStack to support real-time, data-driven invoice operations.
Built the backend with Node.js, Express.js, and Prisma over SQL Server hosted in Azure.
Used Azure Functions for data processing and to run agents that analyze incoming invoices.
Integrated Nylas and Google services to enable controlled access to user email inboxes for invoice ingestion.
Deployed the system in Azure with CI/CD through Azure DevOps.
Used Codex and Mempalace to support project management and software delivery.
Implemented AI-powered invoice analysis using OpenAI.

manual_notes.note: Frontend stack: React.js/Next.js, Tailwind CSS, Lucide React, TanStack, Auth0. Backend stack: Node.js, Express.js, Prisma, SQL Server in Azure, Azure Functions for data processing and invoice-analysis agents. Integrations: Nylas, Google app/email access. DevOps: Azure cloud and Azure DevOps CI/CD. Development tooling: Codex and Mempalace. AI: OpenAI.

source.notes: Parsed from JOSE BANDE SERRANO RESUME V6.pdf
